current_title: Senior Manager, Production Systems & Digital Operations — EV Assembly

education.distinction: Graduated with distinction; thesis on cyber-physical systems in automotive assembly

certifications: Siemens Opcenter MES Key User Certification

career_history.title: Senior Manager, Production Systems & Digital Operations — EV Assembly
career_history.scope: Leads digital production-system integration and performance management routines for EV assembly workstreams; 5 direct reports, 65 indirect project and operational interfaces

key_achievements.achievement: Implemented an integrated digital tier-management routine linking quality deviations, downtime events, and escalation ownership across one EV assembly area
key_achievements.metric: Average escalation closure time reduced from 5.1 days to 2.3 days; repeat issue recurrence reduced 24%

key_achievements.achievement: Supported rollout of a digital deviation tracking logic aligned to BPS escalation standards
key_achievements.metric: Manual reporting workload reduced 31%; shift-leader compliance with standard escalation routines increased from 62% to 91%

career_history.title: Manager, Production Excellence — EV Pilot Operations
career_history.scope: Production-excellence role supporting pilot and pre-series EV operations; cross-functional interface to industrial engineering, quality, and logistics

key_achievements.achievement: Redesigned line-balancing and escalation rules in a pre-series assembly environment
key_achievements.metric: Pilot build schedule adherence improved from 74% to 89%

career_history.title: Graduate Engineer / Process Improvement Specialist
career_history.scope: Early-career roles in process engineering and BPS improvement

key_achievements.achievement: Led takt-time and ergonomics improvements in final assembly
key_achievements.metric: Cycle stability improved 11%; ergonomic incident rate reduced 18%

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Internal move to EV pilot operations

leadership_indicators.leadership_style_self_described: Structured, calm, and systems-oriented. I prefer to make performance visible, reduce ambiguity, and let teams solve problems with clear ownership rather than management drama.
leadership_indicators.decision_making_style: Analytical and relatively careful. Seeks data first, aligns with stakeholders second, and acts once ownership is clear.
leadership_indicators.conflict_handling: Low-drama and controlled. Avoids direct confrontation unless escalation is necessary.
leadership_indicators.change_orientation: Moderately high; comfortable with digital manufacturing change but prefers stepwise implementation over disruptive resets.
leadership_indicators.risk_appetite: Moderate; will pilot new systems but avoids politically exposed bets without sponsor backing.

notable_leadership_moments.situation: A digital escalation rollout stalled because shift leaders viewed it as additional bureaucracy.
notable_leadership_moments.action: Keller simplified the workflow, removed duplicate reporting steps, and ran weekly feedback loops with frontline leaders.
notable_leadership_moments.outcome: Adoption improved materially and the system became embedded rather than bypassed.

performance_reviews.summary: Seen as highly credible in digital production-system work and unusually mature for his level. Development need remains broader people leadership at plant scale.

interview_notes.interviewer: Thomas Brenner, Head of HR Manufacturing; internal talent review panel
interview_notes.overall_impression: Strong internal high-potential with clear credibility in BPS, digital operations, and disciplined execution. Not currently viewed as ready for a full Head of Production mandate, but highly relevant as a future-facing internal benchmark.

question_responses.question: How would you accelerate SOP readiness under timeline compression?
question_responses.response_summary: Focused on decision rights, issue transparency, and line-of-sight accountability rather than heroic firefighting.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Conceptually strong but light on lived executive-scale trade-offs.

interview_notes.case_study_performance: Methodical and structured; good systems answer, less commercial sharpness than more senior candidates.

red_flags: Only 8 years total experience; not credible as a true final-choice candidate without extreme scenario assumptions
Could be perceived as too process-oriented and not decisive enough in crisis conditions
green_flags: Strong BPS and internal cultural fit
Would likely perform well in digital and structured transformation scenarios
